title: Original adaptation of a documentation-heavy product site
productContext: A fictional developer-tools company (product name 'Docuforge') is adapting a publicly accessible documentation site at https://example.com/c2-docs-pilot/ into an original documentation surface. Documentation sites are content-heavy, navigation-critical, and route-dense: every claim must be grounded in the inspected public routes captured in the bound design-source snapshot. The crawl is unauthenticated and non-mutating. The adaptation MUST be original: it may retain or adapt only GENERIC structural principles (information architecture, left-rail navigation, heading hierarchy, code-block treatment) observed in the snapshot; it MUST NOT reproduce the source site's proprietary copy, wordmark, exact palette, unique illustrations, or distinctive iconography. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: developer navigating the documentation to integrate the product
technical writer maintaining the adapted documentation surface
jobs: find the right documentation route via left-rail navigation and search without losing context
read code samples and headings with a clear, retained heading hierarchy and code-block treatment
as a technical writer, author and adapt content within the same generic structural principles
requiredJourneys: developer lands on the migrated documentation home, scans the left-rail navigation, and reaches a representative article route with heading hierarchy intact
developer follows a same-origin internal link between two inspected routes without crossing into an uninspected or blocked route
technical writer perceives a consistent retain/adapt/reject decision across navigation, heading hierarchy, and code-block treatment
constraints: The adaptation MUST be ORIGINAL: it must not reproduce the source site's proprietary copy, wordmark, exact palette, unique illustrations, or distinctive iconography; only generic structural principles observed in the snapshot may be retained or adapted.

Only the routes marked 'inspected' in the bound snapshot may ground a claim; any route marked 'blocked' (e.g., an authenticated admin or draft-content route) MUST be treated as not-observed and no claim may rely on it.

Same-origin internal links between inspected routes must be preserved as a generic principle; cross-origin links or links into blocked routes must be marked clearly and not treated as part of the migrated surface.

A retain / adapt / reject decision MUST be made for every major structural element (navigation, heading hierarchy, code-block treatment, palette, iconography) and recorded explicitly; proprietary identifiers are always 'reject'.
Every claim about the source site must trace to an inspected route in the snapshot; speculative content structure, invented routes, or fabricated API references are forbidden.
The product name Docuforge is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real documentation-platform vendor (e.g., a named docs-as-a-service product) observed or implied by the source.

mobileRules: the left-rail navigation collapses into an operable drawer on narrow viewports and remains keyboard-reachable
the primary search entry stays reachable above the fold on mobile without horizontal scroll
heading hierarchy is preserved as a generic principle and code blocks scroll horizontally inside their own container only
the on-this-page outline remains operable in a single-handed portrait grip
